blocks:
Sūrat al-Muzzammil

Meccan, and it is 20 verses.

Sūrat al-Muzzammil

Meccan. It was said to be Medinan, and it was said that some of it is Meccan and some of it is Medinan. And it is twenty verses. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) يا أَيُّهَا الْمُزَّمِّلُ ** (1) O you wrapped up in your mantle! Its origin is al-mutazzammil (المتزَّمِّل) from tazammala (تزمّل) when he wrapped himself up in it. Al-Qummi said: He is the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, who used to wrap himself in his garment and sleep. So Allah said: ** يا أَيُّهَا الْمُزَّمِّلُ (O you wrapped up in your mantle!).

(٢) قُمِ اللَّيْلَ ** (2) Stand up [for prayer] all night, (Meaning: for prayer) ** إِلَّا قَلِيلاً. (2) except a little,

(٣) نِصْفَهُ أَوِ انْقُصْ مِنْهُ قَلِيلاً. (3) half of it, or diminish a little from it,

(٤) أَوْ زِدْ عَلَيْهِ. (4) or add to it. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The little (القليل) is half, or diminish a little from the little (القليل), or add a little to the little (القليل). And Al-Qummi: what is close to it. ** وَرَتِّلِ الْقُرْآنَ تَرْتِيلاً. (4) and chant the Quran in a measured chant. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and he said: The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "Explain it with clarity (بياناً), and do not rush through it like the rushing of poetry (هذّ الشعر), nor scatter it like the scattering of sand (نثر الرّمل). Rather, empty your hardened hearts, and let not the concern of any of you be the end of the sūrah." The explanation of this hadith and other narrations concerning the meaning of al-tartīl (الترتيل) has already passed in the Eleventh Introduction (المقدّمة الحادية عشرة).

(5) ** إِنَّا سَنُلْقِي عَلَيْكَ قَوْلاً ثَقِيلاً ** (5) (Indeed, We shall cast upon you a weighty word.) It was said: meaning the Qur’an, for due to the obligations (التكاليف) contained within it, it is weighty (ثقيل) upon those who are obligated (المكلّفين). And it was said: meaning the heaviness (ثقيل) of its descent upon him, for his state would change when it descended, and he would sweat.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "The chapter Surat al-Ma'idah descended upon him while He was upon a grayish mule, and the revelation (الوحي) became heavy upon Him until [the mule] fell, and its belly hung down so much that I saw its navel almost touching the ground."

And al-Qummi said regarding قَوْلاً ثَقِيلاً ** (a weighty word): It means standing in prayer during the night (قيام اللّيل), which is His saying: إِنَّ ناشِئَةَ اللَّيْلِ ** the verse.

(٦) إِنَّ ناشِئَةَ اللَّيْلِ ** (Indeed, the rising of the night) It was said: meaning the soul (النّفس) that arises (تنشأ) from its sleeping place (مضجعها) for worship, meaning it rises (تنهض); or the worship (العبادة) that arises (تنشأ) during the night, meaning it occurs (تحدث). ** هِيَ أَشَدُّ وَطْئاً ** meaning greater difficulty (كلفةً) or firmness of footing (ثبات قدم). And it was read (وطأً), meaning the agreement (مواطأة) of the heart (القلب) with the tongue (اللّسان) for it or within it. ** وَأَقْوَمُ قِيلاً ** and more correct (أسدّ) in speech (مقالاً) and more firm (اثبت) in recitation (قراءة), due to the presence of the heart (حضور القلب) and the stillness of the voices (هدوء الأصوات). And al-Qummi said: the most truthful speech (أصدق القول).

In al-Faqih and al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding His saying إِنَّ ناشِئَةَ اللَّيْلِ ** the Ayah, He said: "The rising (قيام) of the man from his bed, intending thereby Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), not intending thereby other than Him." And in a narration: "He intends none but Allah."

And in al-Kafi and al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): that which is similar in meaning.

(٧) إِنَّ لَكَ فِي النَّهارِ سَبْحاً طَوِيلاً. (7) Indeed, there is a long occupation for you during the day. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: "A long period of leisure (فراغاً طويلاً) for your sleep and your need (حاجتك)." (٨) وَاذْكُرِ اسْمَ رَبِّكَ وَتَبَتَّلْ إِلَيْهِ تَبْتِيلاً ** (8) And remember the Name of your Lord, and dedicate yourself to Him with total dedication. And cut yourself off to Him through worship (بالعبادة) and strip your soul bare from everything besides Him. Al-Qummi says: "Be sincere to Him with sincerity (إخلاصاً)." In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: "It is supplication (الدعاء) with a single finger, pointing with it." And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Al-Tabattul (التبتّل) is gesturing (الإيماء) with the finger. In al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): Indeed, al-Tabattul (التبتّل) here is raising the hands (رفع اليدين) during the prayer (الصلاة). And in a narration: It is raising your hands to Allah and your humility (تضرّعك) before Him. In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): Al-Tabattul (التبتّل) is turning over your palms (كفّيك) during supplication (الدعاء) when you supplicate. And Al-Qummi said: Raising the hands (رفع اليدين) and moving the two index fingers (السّبّابتين).

(٩) رَبُّ الْمَشْرِقِ وَالْمَغْرِبِ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ فَاتَّخِذْهُ وَكِيلاً. (9) The Lord of the east and the west, there is no god except Him. So take Him for a trustee. (١٠) وَاصْبِرْ عَلى ما يَقُولُونَ. (10) And be patient with what they say. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), He said: [Be patient with] what they say about you. وَاهْجُرْهُمْ هَجْراً جَمِيلاً (And forsake them with a graceful forsaking) means that you avoid them (تُجَانِبُهُمْ), treat them gently (تُدَارِيهِمْ), and entrust their affair to Allah. (١١) وَذَرْنِي وَالْمُكَذِّبِينَ ** (11) And leave Me and the deniers. Leave Me and them, and entrust their affair to Me, for I am sufficient without you in recompensing them. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): وَالْمُكَذِّبِينَ ** (And the deniers) of your testament (وصيّتك). It was said: This is a revelation (تنزيل). He said: Yes. أُولِي النَّعْمَةِ (those possessing ease) means the masters of luxury (أَرْبَابُ التَّنَعُّمِ). وَمَهِّلْهُمْ قَلِيلاً (And respite them a little). In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a hadith wherein he mentions the hypocrites (المنافقين). He said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم continued to reconcile with them (يَتَأَلَّفُهُمْ), draw them near, and seat them to His right and His left, until Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, permitted Him to distance them by His saying: وَاهْجُرْهُمْ هَجْراً جَمِيلاً. (And forsake them with a graceful forsaking.)

(١٢) إِنَّ لَدَيْنا أَنْكالاً ** (12) Indeed, with Us are fetters This is a justification for the command (or warning). And the nukl (النكل) is the heavy shackle. وَجَحِيماً. (and a blazing fire.) (١٣) وَطَعاماً ذا غُصَّةٍ ** (13) and food that chokes, Food that sticks in the throat, like al-Ḍarīʿ (الضّريع) and al-Zaqqūm (الزقّوم). وَعَذاباً أَلِيماً ** (and a painful punishment.) And another type of painful punishment, the essence of which is known only to Allah. And it was interpreted as deprivation from meeting Allah, because the rebellious souls immersed in desires remain shackled by their love for them and their attachment to them, preventing them from escaping to the realm of Holiness (عالم القدس), burning with the anguish of separation, swallowing the choking bitterness (غصّة) of estrangement (الهجران), and punished by deprivation from the manifestation (تجلّي) of the lights of Holiness (أنوار القدس). In al-Majmaʿ (المجمع), narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: that He heard a reciter reciting it (the verse) and He swooned (صعق).

(١٤) يَوْمَ تَرْجُفُ الْأَرْضُ وَالْجِبالُ (14) the day the earth and the mountains will quake, They will shake and be convulsed (تَزَلْزَل). And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) (said): they will sink (تَخْسِف). وَكانَتِ الْجِبالُ كَثِيباً مَهِيلاً and the mountains will be a heap of sliding sand. He said: like sand, they will slide down (تَنْحَدِر). (١٥) إِنَّا أَرْسَلْنا إِلَيْكُمْ رَسُولاً شاهِداً عَلَيْكُمْ (15) Indeed, We have sent to you an apostle, a witness against you, He will bear witness against you on the Day of Resurrection regarding acceptance (of the message) and refusal (الِامْتِنَاع), كَما أَرْسَلْنا إِلى فِرْعَوْنَ رَسُولاً just as We sent an apostle to Pharaoh. Meaning Musa (peace be upon him), and He did not specify him because the intended purpose was not related to him.

(١٦) فَعَصى فِرْعَوْنُ الرَّسُولَ فَأَخَذْناهُ أَخْذاً وَبِيلاً ** (16) But Pharaoh disobeyed the Apostle, so We seized him with a terrible seizing. severe (ثقيلاً). (١٧) فَكَيْفَ تَتَّقُونَ إِنْ كَفَرْتُمْ يَوْماً يَجْعَلُ الْوِلْدانَ شِيباً ** (17) So, if you disbelieve, how will you guard yourselves against a day which will turn the children gray? due to the severity of its terror. Al-Qummi narrated: Due to the fright (الفزع) when they hear the cry (الصّيحة). He said: He says, "How will you guard yourselves against that Day if you disbelieve?" (١٨) السَّماءُ مُنْفَطِرٌ بِهِ (١) cleaving (منشقّ) كانَ وَعْدُهُ مَفْعُولاً. (18) The sky will be split open by it; His promise is bound to be fulfilled. (١٩) إِنَّ هذِهِ ** (19) This is indeed a reminder. So let anyone who wishes take the way toward his Lord. These promised signs (الآيات الموعدة) تَذْكِرَةٌ ** an admonition (عظة) فَمَنْ شاءَ اتَّخَذَ إِلى رَبِّهِ سَبِيلاً ** meaning, he draws near to Him by following the path of piety (التقوى). (٢٠) إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَعْلَمُ أَنَّكَ تَقُومُ أَدْنى مِنْ ثُلُثَيِ اللَّيْلِ وَنِصْفَهُ وَثُلُثَهُ ** (20) Indeed your Lord knows that you stand vigil for nearly two-thirds of the night, or half of it, or a third of it, and a group of those who are with you. Allah determines the measure of the night and the day. He knew that you would not calculate it, so He relented toward you. So recite as much of the Qur’an as is feasible. He knew that there will be among you the sick, and others travelling in the land, seeking Allah’s grace, and others fighting in the way of Allah. So recite as much of it as is feasible, and maintain the prayer and give the zakat and lend Allah a good loan. Whatever good you send ahead for your souls, you will find it with Allah as better and greater in reward. And plead with Allah for forgiveness. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. It was recited (وقرئ) wa-niṣfahu wa-thuluthahu (وَنِصْفَهُ وَثُلُثَهُ) with the naṣb (accusative case). وَطائِفَةٌ مِنَ الَّذِينَ مَعَكَ وَاللهُ يُقَدِّرُ اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهارَ None knows the measures of their hours as they truly are except Allah. عَلِمَ أَنْ لَنْ تُحْصُوهُ ** meaning, that you would not calculate the estimation of the times (تقدير الأوقات) or that you would not be able to precisely determine the hours (ضبط السّاعات). فَتابَ عَلَيْكُمْ ** by granting permission (بالترخيص) to abandon the prescribed standing (القيام المقدّر) and lifting the burden (التبعة) regarding it. فَاقْرَؤُا ما تَيَسَّرَ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ meaning, pray with whatever reading (القراءة) is made easy for you.

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Rida, from his father, from his grandfather (peace be upon them), he said: "What is feasible (ما تيسّر) of it is that in which you have humility of the heart (خشوع القلب) and purity of the inner self (صفاء السرّ)."

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding His Almighty saying: إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَعْلَمُ أَنَّكَ تَقُومُ أَدْنى مِنْ ثُلُثَيِ اللَّيْلِ وَنِصْفَهُ وَثُلُثَهُ ** The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم did that and gave the people glad tidings of it, but it became difficult for them. And عَلِمَ أَنْ لَنْ تُحْصُوهُ ** (He knew that you would not calculate it). A man would stand in prayer and not know when half the night was, nor when two-thirds of it was. And a man would stand in prayer until morning, fearing that he would not preserve it (the time limit). So Allah revealed: إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَعْلَمُ أَنَّكَ تَقُومُ ** up to His saying: عَلِمَ أَنْ لَنْ تُحْصُوهُ ** (He knew that you would not calculate it), meaning, when the half and the third would be. This verse was abrogated (نسخت) by: ** فَاقْرَؤُا ما تَيَسَّرَ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ ** (So recite as much of the Qur’an as is feasible). And know that no prophet ever came except that he devoted himself to the night prayer (صلاة اللّيل), and no prophet ever came with the night prayer (صلاة اللّيل) being performed at the beginning of the night. عَلِمَ أَنْ سَيَكُونُ مِنْكُمْ مَرْضى ** This is a new sentence (استئناف) explaining another wisdom necessitating the permission (الترخيص) and the alleviation (التخفيف). وَآخَرُونَ يَضْرِبُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ يَبْتَغُونَ مِنْ فَضْلِ اللهِ journeying for trade and the acquisition of knowledge (تحصيل العلم). وَآخَرُونَ يُقاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ فَاقْرَؤُا ما تَيَسَّرَ مِنْهُ وَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلاةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكاةَ وَأَقْرِضُوا اللهَ قَرْضاً حَسَناً ** He means by this all other expenditures (الانفاقات) in the way of goodness. Al-Qummi said: It is other than the Zakat (الزكاة). وَما تُقَدِّمُوا لِأَنْفُسِكُمْ مِنْ خَيْرٍ تَجِدُوهُ عِنْدَ اللهِ هُوَ خَيْراً ** meaning, you will find it good (تجدوه خيراً). The pronoun (الضمير) هُوَ is for separation (الفصل) and support (العماد). It is also said that it is an attribute (صفة) for the hā' (pronoun) in تَجِدُوهُ وَأَعْظَمَ أَجْراً ** (and greater in reward) than that which you postpone until the bequest (الوصيّة) at death, or than the enjoyment (متاع) of this world. ** وَاسْتَغْفِرُوا اللهَ ** in all your circumstances, for you are not free from negligence (تفريط). إِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ. (Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.)

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever recites Surat al-Muzzammil during the last evening prayer (العشاء الآخرة) or at the end of the night, the night and the day will be witnesses for him, along with Surat al-Muzzammil, and Allah will grant him a good life (حياة طيبة) and cause him to die a good death (ميتة طيبة)."

footnote (١) The pronoun (*الهاء*) refers back to the Day (*اليوم*), and this is like saying, 'So-and-so is in Kufa.'
